summary: ANDROID 🟥 Incorrect device state displayed after prolonged app idle; wrong headset shown as connected and settings accessible
scenario: After extended idle period, app on launch shows stale earbud connection state (left connected, right disconnected) and incorrectly allows access to settings, requiring app restart to show correct disconnected state.
description_clean: After the app is idle for an extended period (e.g., overnight), the device state is not refreshed correctly on launch. Earbuds are shown as partially connected (left connected, right disconnected) even though both are in the case and fully disconnected in system Bluetooth. The actually connected device (Surround 80) is not displayed. The app also incorrectly allows access to earbuds settings when they are disconnected; toggling settings produces no response. The correct state only appears after restarting the app.
steps: Leave the app idle for an extended period (e.g., overnight).
Check the system Bluetooth settings to verify actual connection status.
Attempt to access earbuds settings from the app.
Toggle any setting within the earbuds settings.
Observe the displayed device state after restart.
expected: On launch, the app should correctly reflect the actual device state: both earbuds should show as disconnected (since both are in the case and fully disconnected in system Bluetooth), and the actually connected device (Surround 80) should be displayed. Access to earbuds settings should be blocked when earbuds are disconnected.
actual: On launch, earbuds are shown as partially connected (left connected, right disconnected) even though both are in the case and fully disconnected in system Bluetooth. The actually connected device (Surround 80) is not displayed. The app incorrectly allows access to earbuds settings when they are disconnected; toggling settings produces no response. The correct state only appears after restarting the app.
